message:
test(readme): cover Series statistical + inspection methods (fd90.231 / br-vhr55)

Extends readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover 13
Series-level methods at fp-frame:3311-5233 that previously had no
integration coverage:

f64 statistics:
- skew, kurt, kurtosis, sem

usize position lookups:
- argmax, argmin

bool structural inspection:
- is_unique
- is_monotonic_increasing / is_monotonic_decreasing

bool reductions (on a Bool Series):
- all, any

Series-level top/bottom-N:
- nlargest(3) / nsmallest(2)

Built two helper Series — a 6-element float Series for the stats and
position lookups, and a 3-element Bool Series for the bool reductions.

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -849,6 +849,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-v9po","title":"Add series_rolling_var/count and ewm_mean and resample_sum/mean/count edge cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:28:22.967980657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:29:15.952839036Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:29:15.951054829Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-vc9cp","title":"fd90.162: README random sampling example calls non-existent compute_weight(i) — replace with inline expression","description":"fd90.74-161 self-review. The Random Sampling example at line 1641 (added by fd90.115) has:

  let weights: Vec<f64> = (0..df.len()).map(|i| compute_weight(i)).collect();

The compute_weight function doesn't exist anywhere — it was a placeholder for 'user-defined weight function'. A user copy-pasting this hits:
  error[E0425]: cannot find function  in this scope

This is an outlier among the README's code examples — most others compile. Fix: replace with an inline weight expression (e.g. positional weighting where row index becomes the weight) so the example is genuinely runnable. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:49:30.931231782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:50:47.796752498Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:50:47.793932948Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-vhq2","title":"fd90.20: SqlConnection::list_tables for backend-agnostic table discovery","description":"Add SqlConnection::list_tables(schema: Option<&str>) -> Vec<String> trait method for backend-agnostic table discovery. Default impl returns empty Vec (backends that can't introspect). rusqlite impl uses 'SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type=table' filtered by schema (silently ignored on SQLite per supports_schemas==false). Free-fn list_sql_tables(conn, schema) wrapper exposes the API at fp-io top level. Tests cover empty DB, populated DB, schema-Some on SQLite (silently ignored), schema-Some on a multi-schema stub backend.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T20:21:10.294844156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T20:24:29.991628127Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T20:24:29.989102037Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-vhr55","title":"fd90.231: Cover Series stat + inspection methods (skew/kurt/argmax/is_unique/etc.)","description":"fd90.231: Cover Series statistical / inspection methods.

Series has 11+ statistical and structural-inspection methods at fp-frame:3311-5233 with no integration coverage:

Higher-order statistics (return f64):
- skew, kurt, kurtosis, sem (lines 5038-5093)

Position lookups (return usize):
- argmax, argmin (lines 4788-4809)

Boolean inspection (return bool):
- is_unique, is_monotonic_increasing, is_monotonic_decreasing
- hasnans (already covered minimally)

Boolean reductions (return bool):
- all, any (lines 4073-4088)

Top/bottom-N:
- Series.nlargest(n) / Series.nsmallest(n) — already covered for DataFrame in fd90.185 but Series-level is not exercised

Scope:
- Extend an existing test (readme_element_wise_operations) with all calls.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
